cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about how long the experiment lasted according to the text. Looking at the text, the study involved participants writing for three consecutive days, 20 minutes each day. Then, two weeks after the third day of writing, they had a skin biopsy. The wounds were monitored over the following days. The results mentioned that on the 11th day after the biopsy, the wounds had healed for some. Wait, the experiment duration might be a bit confusing here. The writing part was three days, but the healing process was observed over time. The key part is when they say "two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a biopsy." So the experiment wasn't just the three days of writing. The healing was tracked for 11 days after the biopsy. So from the start of the writing (three days) to the biopsy (two weeks later), then 11 days of monitoring. But the question is asking for the duration of the experiment. The three days of writing, plus two weeks until the biopsy, then 11 days of monitoring. But the answer options are about the total duration. The text says the experiment included the writing phase and the healing monitoring. The critical part is when they say "Two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a standard 4mm skin biopsy... The wounds were photographed regularly over the following days..." and "On the 11th day after the biopsy..." So the experiment likely lasted about 11 days after the biopsy (which was two weeks after the writing). So total duration would be two weeks plus 11 days. But the answer options are about the experiment's total length. Wait, but the question is phrased as "the experiment lasted"—probably the answer is the time from when the participants started writing until the end of the monitoring. So three days writing, then two weeks until the biopsy, then 11 days of monitoring. That's 3 days + 14 days + 11
